entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: The word is vitarkitah pura buddhya — deliberated first, in the mind — and nivesitah, installed, upon its meaning

entities.name: buddhi
entities.cite: The word is vitarkitah pura buddhya — deliberated first, in the mind

entities.name: artha
entities.cite: and nivesitah, installed, upon its meaning before a single sound occurs

entities.name: dhvani
entities.cite: the inner word anugrhyate, is *favoured* or assisted, by the dhvani

entities.name: karaṇa
entities.cite: Only then do the karanas, the articulatory organs, come into play

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: and nivesitah, installed, upon its meaning before a single sound occurs

axioms.name: vitarkita
axioms.cite: The word is vitarkitah pura buddhya — deliberated first, in the mind

axioms.name: anugrhyate
axioms.cite: the inner word anugrhyate, is *favoured* or assisted, by the dhvani

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: what the organs produce must come out in sequence because the organs work in time

denials.name: is_substance
denials.cite: Sound is an auxiliary, a helper of manifestation, not the substance of the word

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: unrolled from the organs of speech

rejected_readings.label: sonic_primacy
rejected_readings.rendering: Sound is the fundamental nature of the word, produced by the organs of speech and then given meaning by the intellect.
rejected_readings.why: Inverts the ontological order and contradicts the commentary's key point that sound is auxiliary, not the substance of the word. The word is first established as meaningful before sound appears.

rejected_readings.label: simultaneity
rejected_readings.rendering: The intellect and the organs of speech work together to produce the word and its sound at the same time.
rejected_readings.why: Collapses the temporal order that the verse carefully stages: first the meaningful conception in the mind, then the sonic unfold from the organs. The commentary emphasizes this sequencing as crucial to understanding the word's nature.

notes: The verse encodes the dual nature of the word: inner (meaningful, mental, prior in order) and outer (sonic, temporal, auxiliary). Four axioms capture: (1) meaning as basis, (2) intellect as locus of deliberation, (3) sound as auxiliary to the word, (4) organs as source of sonic manifestation. The denial anchors the key philosophical point: sound assists but is not the substance of the word.
